label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me work through this step by step. So the question is whether following a set of instructions brings me back to the starting point. The instructions are: 1. Turn around. 2. Turn left. 3. Take 1 step. 4. Take 2 steps. 5. Turn around. 6. Turn around. 7. Turn around. 8. Take 3 steps. First, I need to track my movements and direction changes. Let me visualize this as moving on a grid, starting at (0,0) facing north. Let's see how each instruction affects my position and direction. Let me define the directions: - North (up, y-axis positive) - East (right, x-axis positive) - South (down, y-axis negative) - West (left, x-axis negative) A "turn around" means a 180-degree turn, so facing the opposite direction. A "turn left" is a 90-degree counter-clockwise turn. Starting position: (0,0), facing North. 1. Turn around: Now facing South. 2. Turn left: From South, a left turn is East (counter-clockwise). So now facing East. 3. Take 1 step: Move 1 step East to (1,0). 4. Take 2 steps: Still facing East, so move 2 steps to (3,0). 5. Turn around: Now facing West. 6. Turn around: Now facing East. 7. Turn around: Now facing West. 8. Take 3 steps: Facing West, so move 3 steps to (0,0). Wait, let me check step 5-7 again. After step 4, I'm at (3,0), facing East. 5. Turn around: Facing West. 6. Turn around: Facing East again. 7. Turn around: Facing West again. 8. Take 3 steps: From (3,0), facing West, moving 3 steps would take me to (0,0). So yes, I return to the starting point. But wait, let me double-check each turn. Starting from East: - Turn around (step 5): East ->
